description: This figure presents EBSD characterization results of the necking region after tensile fracture for three Fe-0.25C-3Mn-2Al-0.4Si low-density steel samples: AN750-T (750 °C annealed + 400 °C aged), AN800-T (800 °C annealed + 400 °C aged), AN850-T (850 °C annealed + 400 °C aged). Subfigure (a) shows Kikuchi pattern quality and phase distribution maps, with retained austenite (RA, red marked) fractions of 0.018, 0.004, 0.002 for the three samples respectively, decreasing with increasing annealing temperature. Subfigure (b) displays magnified Kikuchi pattern quality and KAM maps, marking soft ferrite (F) domains, hard regions (B/TM/FM, red ellipses), and white pixel regions prone to micropore nucleation during deformation. Subfigure (c) plots point-to-point (P-T-P) orientation difference (black curves, average values 3.50°, 4.48°, 3.50° for the three samples) and Kikuchi pattern quality (red curves) along selected lines. Sharp orientation difference peaks and Kikuchi quality drops at phase boundaries confirm strong strain and dislocation accumulation at phase interfaces, supporting that heterogeneous soft-hard domain interfaces induce stress redistribution to accommodate deformation discontinuity.
